title: process_image_bytes re-encodes everything as PNG but mime_type is original format
description: In src/media.rs, process_image_bytes() always calls img.write_to(&mut cursor, image::ImageFormat::Png), so the stored file is always a PNG regardless of input format. However, sniff_image_mime() returns the original MIME type (e.g. image/jpeg for a JPEG), which is then bound into the post_media row and used in the ActivityPub attachment's mediaType field. The resulting state: a .jpg file containing PNG bytes with a mediaType of image/jpeg. Remote clients decoding based on mediaType will misidentify the format. Fix: either always store as PNG and set mime_type to image/png, or re-encode to the original format and preserve its MIME type.
close_reason: All pass 4 fixes applied: media SSRF+size guard, mime/format consistency, proper keyId parsing, fail-closed sig verify, path reconstruction, key owner validation, media cap, blocking stdin fix, conflicts_with
